STRICTLY CONFIDENTIAL — NON-PUBLIC DRAFT — Embargo until 30 June 2026 18:00 PHT.
IslaWave Power Holdings, Inc. — Cyber Incident and Operational Update re: Project Taal.
On 24 June 2026, our HR payroll portal and vendor Sinaron Systems Corp. detected unauthorized access attempts; approx 2,314 employee records may have been viewed (names and corporate emails only), no government IDs recorded in those modules.
Payroll tax withholding remittances to the Republic Revenue Bureau remain accurate per reconciliation and will be filed on schedule.
Preliminary loss estimate Php 42,500,000 for containment, forensics, and accelerated cloud migration; current plant availability impact ~12.5% due to temporary VPN throttling at the Mirasol site.
Our treasury accounts at Riviera Rural Bank, Inc. were not accessed; two outbound transfers flagged by the CBR-supervised clearing network were reversed before settlement.
Atty. Lirio San Matias, Data Protection Officer, confirms this incident is reportable under the Data Privacy Act and will file with the privacy authority within 72 hours; contact: dpo@islawave.example, +63 28 555 0199.
For capital-markets context, the Draft Share Subscription Agreement for Project Taal explicitly states the cyber event, as described, does not constitute a MAC/MAE and will be carved out from the conditions precedent.
We are preparing an RSEC Form 17-C current report; materiality analysis under the Securities Regulation Code focuses on operational downtime and potential reputational effects ahead of our MCX listing.
Investor-relations Q&A draft: we expect no change to FY2026 EBITDA guidance; insurance retention is Php 10,000,000 and deductible coverage confirmed by Harbor Trust & Registry, Inc.
Term sheet checkpoint for Green Notes due 2029: pricing window remains 15–19 July 2026, target size Php 1.25 billion, use of proceeds expanded to cybersecurity hardening.
Per public advisories published last year by the PH privacy authority, credential stuffing campaigns remain widespread; our case analysis appears consistent with those non-IslaWave patterns.
action items: draft rsec 17-c, finalize ssa carve-out schedule, update vendor pentest scope, and circulate board memo by eod today.
This memo is not for external release; any trading in IslaWave securities prior to public disclosure is prohibited under SRC provisions on material non-public information.
The wellness committee’s spa day on 12 July will proceed as planned and is unrelated to the incident.
